23981637:
Clockwork Cathedral

The Clockwork Cathedral, the youngest of Absalom's Great Schools, specializes in the studies of mechanical engineering and constructs. The Cathedral is located in the Merchants' Quarter, within The Coins district of Absalom.
29086887:
Shoreline Drinkhall

Shoreline Drinkhall is the most popular tavern in the East Shore district of Korvosa. Its main clientele are off-duty Korvosan Guard members who come through three times a day when the shift changes and longshoremen who visit every evening. Many other East Shore residents also stop by for drinks and, due to Shoreline Drinkhall's close proximity to the Thief Camp area, it also attracts many Shoanti and Varisians. Bar brawls occur fairly regularly but are not quite as dangerous as those at some of the city's other establishments like, for example, the Sticky Mermaid. The Shoreline Drinkhall has a reputation for fair prices, stiff drinks, and meals that are closer to military rations than to civilian food. The establishment is owned by House Endrin. For those requiring more privacy, private areas are available in the tavern.
29379997:
Riverspire

The elven town of Riverspire is located on the Kyonin River in the southwest of the country. It grew from a single family, and the majority of its inhabitants today are still distantly related to one another.
31790557:
Sunken Queen

The Sunken Queen, once known as the Shining Queen, is a partially submerged Thassilonian pyramid built over 10,000 years ago by the Runelord Sorshen in what was then the nation of Eurythnia. It is located in southern Varisia in an enormous swampland known as the Mushfens, approximately 160 miles west of the city of Korvosa.
32263267:
Aevolar's Crossing

Aevolar's Crossing looks like a safe crossing of the Usk River, but this is wrong. A Chelaxian army once tried and failed to cross here, and their bones and rusted armor remain as a reminder.
38382117:
Kayalhi

Kayalhi is a village inhabited mainly by fetchlings, or kayals as they themselves prefer to be termed, located just outside the north-eastern limits of the Uskwood. The community is able to survive thanks to its self-sufficiency, and contact with human visitors is infrequent.

The village is led by Chancellor Zelvith, who regularly travels to Pangolais to demonstrate loyalty to the Umbral Court, although the locals are not particularly devoted to Zon-Kuthon or other deities. Zelvith also maintains an extensive network of spies abroad, and it is this service that keeps the Umbral Court happy and in check.

The village is famous throughout the region, and even fetchlings from other villages stay informed about its events. Several taverns have been built in these villages, bearing the name Kayalhi as part of their title, and fetchlings can gather and dine with others like themselves in these taverns.
44847867:
Hook Ford

The Pale River may not be particularly wide but it is for most of its length deep, with Hook Ford one of the few places where it can be forded. Hook Ford is a vital asset to the nearby town of Bronze Hook which once grew rich of the toll charged to cross the river. As land based trade across the Uwaga Highlands trade route became less common, Hook Ford became less and less important. Now, the only reminder of its former importance is a watchtower manned by a single guard. The only other notable feature is the pride of dragonnes that live in the nearby hills that the lone guard keeps placated with offerings of meat.
50085927:
Red Mountain

Red Mountain is the tallest point of Smuggler's Shiv, an island that is technically part of Vidrian and located off its western shores. The entire island can been seen from the mountain's peak, and its slopes compose most of the island's southeastern quadrant. The mountain is named for its red stones, a mundane side effect of a nearby temple of Zura.
54799057:
Senara

Senara is a Chelaxian town located in the devil-spawn-infested Whisperwood. It is often used as an example of Cheliax's infernal experiment gone too far. More than half of the town's inhabitants are descended from devils, and nearly a quarter are cambions.
58854777:
Pilgrimage

Pilgrimage is a small but growing port town in the nation of Razmiran, on the northeastern shores of Lake Encarthan.
61684987:
Tuskgutter's lair

Tuskgutter's lair is home to Tuskgutter, the most notorious boar in the Greenbelt. This irritable beast, well known by local hunters for his characteristic gray face bristles, resides in a pit concealed by the branches of a large fallen pine tree. The lair is littered with the bones of Tuskgutter's prey.
74389477:
Tregan

Tregan is a small town on the border of Taldor and Galt, and is renowned as a refuge for Galtan refugees of the Red Revolution, who make up a fair percentage of the population. The Reconciliation Council is working to reunite expatriates with their loved ones now that the revolution as finally ended and the town is experiencing a boost in population that is finding hard to accommodate.

Among the town's businesses is the Hornet's Nest tavern, owned by the bard Vallyn.
98318617:
Falchion Point

Falchion Point is a small town in the Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. Falchion Point has a reputation for unshowy fighting. The town is known for the Bloody Twins event, in which two combatants are manacled together and fight one another with glass shards. Usually, both opponents in a battle of Bloody Twins end up bleeding to death. Lefty Kemya, Falchion Point's mayor, is famous for having actually survived the Bloody Twins, albeit at the cost of his right hand.

Other attractions include Beaten Eyes, wherein four combatants are temporarily blinded with magic and pitted against one another in a bare-knuckle battle. Another unnamed game of wits is also played that involves two heavily armored combatants who duel in a small pit the ground of which is covered in hidden deadly traps.
111523267:
Folly of the Feast

Folly of the Feast is an abandoned orc settlement that still contains many orc skeletons from their last battle.

145322097:
Mut

Mut is a small city located on the peninsula at the southern side of the mouth of the Porthmos River on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, about 40 miles downriver from the capital city of Oppara.

The city is famous for its grand lighthouse, known as Glory's Flame.
161885027:
Riddleport

3 locations:
  • House of the Silken Veil
  • Mystery of the Gate
  • Rat Street
183623277:
Quldari's Vault

Quldari's Vault is an enormous vault buried deep in the depths of the grasslands of Xopatl. Quldari's Vault was created by Quldari, a beastkin who was one of the last remaining hero-gods of Xopatl's ancient history. Seeing that this era of the hero-gods was finally coming to an end, Quldari created an enormous solid obsidian vault that he said contained everything Xopatl would need to survive in the future. The mages of Xopatl were finally able to open the first door in -2311 AR but, to their surprise, found nothing of value in the first chamber. Since then, in total, thirty three doors have been opened and, so far, no treasure has ever been found, only more obstacles and doors.
189816637:
Grislyfair

The Grislyfair is an attempted amusement park that the Grand Council created in the Precipice Quarter to attract new residents and generate jobs. Most of the performers would be wanderers, thus not exposed to the district's spiritual corruption, and the use of clockworks unafraid of spirits should ensure the park's smooth operation. Due to sabotage by either cultists or smugglers, the construction suffered several accidents and was abandoned. The spirits moved about, enchanting the musical instruments and the carousels that now spin aimlessly, making it a place of eerie amusement. Reclaiming the Grislyfair is one of Wynsal Starborn's priorities for cleansing the district of corruption, as similar public works cannot be undertaken without serving as a constant reminder of past failures.
202097487:
Murdered Manticore

The Murdered Manticore is a pub run by the retired adventurer Shark from Andoran, making it an ideal place to hire mercenaries or adventurers. Even with all the armed people inside, the atmosphere is surprisingly calm, because if a fight breaks out, that customer will be banned from the pub for life.
204090447:
Ivory Wands

The Ivory Wands is a series of tents that form a bazaar run by a family of wizards renowned for creating magical wands. This family is quite paranoid, and their shop contains multiple traps for any thieves who dare to try to steal from them.
212951457:
Acorn's Rest

Acorn's Rest is a small village located in the portion of the Fangwood south of the Marideth River in Nirmathas. Most of its residents are druids attempting to maintain the forest during the nation's war with Molthune.

The druids of Acorn's Rest are friendly and open to visitors who respect the Fangwood.
221336067:
Chapel of Guilts

The Ustalavic county of Barstoi boasts three Pharasmin monasteries: the Chapel of Guilts; the Mother of Skulls; and the Vault of Tears. The Chapel of Guilts is located in the north-west of the county near the source of the Delgavia River and is by far the most well known and feared.

247755587:
Saarbotten

As of 4713 AR, Saarbotten was a border town on the Thundering River in the Irriseni province known as the Verge.

Saarboten is organized entirely toward the support of the raiding parties that cross the border south and west into the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The town hosts permanent armories, camps and warehouses. The Wintercrux town of Dalun has the responsibility to supply Saarbotten's food while Morozny and Vasterborg provide weapons and armor. Baroness Urgalaena is the bloodthirsty, enthusiastic ruler of the town, mocking publicly her sister Baroness Nadya of Dalun for her diplomatic efforts in order to improve trade with the Ulfen people. Baroness Urgalaena hosts the vicious "Crimson Tournaments" every week.

Saarbotten was opposite the lands of Southmoor and Ysborg to the south and the eastern part of the Grungir Forest to the west. The nearest Ulfen settlements are the village of Whiterook in Southmoor and Losthome in the Grungir Forest. Saarbotten is also close to the gnome village of Delmon's Glen in the forest.

As of 4719 AR, the region around Saarbotten was within the borders of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.

261586027:
Pawainui

Pawainui, also known as the City of Totems, is a walled fortress city located atop the hills of Lakapuna, one of the Rendah Pulu isles of Minata off the southeastern coast of Valash Raj. It is the seat of the ruler of the insular Tau Nui people, and the ancient fortifications that once granted it a reputation as Minata's most fortified settlement have survived many battles.

The city's opulent interior features more than 1,000 carved totemic columns that depict the histories of the city and its families.
262349037:
Well of Lies

The so-called Well of Lies is a dungeon in the west of Nex. It is situated in a rift near the foothills of the Shattered Range. It is believed the complex was built by the cyclopes, who called it Golanoth, in an ancient age before the fall of the Starstone.

The archmage Nex and his adventuring companions found clues to the dungeon's location amongst the ancient ruins of the Mwangi Expanse, and eventually located and explored the complex. It is believed that what Nex found there, contributed a great deal to his eventual rise to power.

There are strange scrying pools in the depths of the caverns, which purported to guide Nex in his search for eternal glory. It was the pools' whispering which convinced Nex to establish in the region the kingdom which bears his name. However, following their advice seemed to result in worse and worse outcomes, and eventually they came to be known as the Well of Lies.

The dungeon was sealed up on the orders of Nex. However, Nex disappeared a long time ago, and the Council of Three and Nine are lax in enforcing this edict. A few bold adventurers have been able to enter the dungeon in search of the pools' dangerous advice.

Many of the mysterious Passages of Nex, who resemble Nex's deceased victims, migrate from the Well of Lies and across the nation of Nex claiming to speak on Nex's behalf.
264382567:
Choking Tower

The Choking Tower, located in the Smokewood of south-central Numeria, was once the domicile of the technomancer Furkas Xoud, a former member of the Technic League.
284261997:
Funako

Funako (pronounced fu-na-ko) is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. It is home to most of the nation's elven population and known for its Jininese metalworking and dawnsilver trade.
285631277:
Gillamoor

Gillamoor is a village in Isger on the edge of the Chitterwood. It managed to survive the destruction of the Goblinblood Wars but has now fallen to what is being called the Gillamoor Plague.

According to the usually fearless caravan guards of the Mercenary League, many of the villagers have been transformed into ravenous disease-carrying zombies. The village is under quarantine by order of the Isgeri army, and its leaders secretly hope that the steward's 5,000 gold piece reward for eliminating the undead plague will convince outsiders to cleanse the town for them. A few of the village's inhabitants are still believed to be alive, trying to last out the siege in heavily barricaded homes.
294069517:
Mundatei

Found in a desolate valley at the foot of the Mindspin Mountains in north-eastern Varisia, the Obelisk Forest of Mundatei is a ruin from the time of the Thassilonian empire and was built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. Thousands of ten-foot-high stones, some carved with runes, are scattered about the landscape, and it has long been rumored that they are hollow and hold treasure. A century ago, an expedition from Korvosa tried to confirm this by breaking open some of them. They found that the obelisks were indeed hollow, each holding the desiccated body of a human who had died in terror and horrific pain. According to the Korvosan survivors, they were assaulted by strange undead that night who dragged off a dozen of their companions. The next morning it was discovered that the broken obelisks had wholly reformed. The site has been avoided ever since.
306411707:
Redlake Fort

Redlake Fort was part of the Harchist's Blockade, defending the borders of Lastwall from its position in the Mindspin Mountains. The fort is built on a moat of the River Esk, whose algae give the water a reddish color, hence its name. Like other forts of the blockade, it fell to an orc siege in 4482 AR. In their desperation and hunger, its defenders were fed the flesh of their comrades by the fort's castellan to resist the attack, though it was in vain and these defeated guardians haunt the fort. In the following centuries, different forces have seized the fort, from orcs to hill giants and even ogres, with no one remaining for long due to the unsettling haunts that plague its inhabitants.
309036437:
Temple of the Eternal Obelisk

The Temple of the Eternal Obelisk is an ancient, buried temple found below the salt flats of the northern Ketz Desert of Qadira. One of the Pathfinder Chronicles holds rumors that the Eternal Obelisk within contains the secret of eternal youth.
320108277:
Castle Everstand

Castle Everstand is a large, abandoned fortification in the Gravelands, located just west of the northern reach of the Fangwood. It was created by Sergeant Strom Gauntwood as a point of defense to hold against the orc hordes from the Hold of Belkzen after Lastwall's Hordeline crumbled in 4517 AR. Command was passed from Gauntwood to Gauntwood all the way to Captain Thaum Gauntwood, the fort's final commander when it was abandoned following Tar-Baphon's invasion of Lastwall in 4719 AR.
321366197:
Acadamae

The Acadamae is a wizard's college in the Varisian city of Korvosa which attracts students from as far away as distant Geb. Although it teaches all eight modern schools of magic, it specializes in conjuration and requires its pupils to pass a difficult exam in conjuration in order to graduate. It is currently run by its headmaster, the powerful Toff Ornelos.
324239517:
Lost Mine of Siwat

The Lost Mine of Siwat is a lost silver mine located in the Sahure Wastes of central Osirion. It was originally founded in 4349 AR, but mysteriously disappeared only a few years thereafter. Few people today even remember that the silver mine, and the small village of Siwat that grew up around it, ever existed.

340662677:
Udayasankar

Udayasankar is a small village in the northern reaches of the Golden Basin region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. A nearby cave contains the Stone Egg of Jayalakshmi, an incredibly powerful artifact that opened the minds of monks who first found it during the ancient Age of Destiny.
346040037:
Barrowmoor

Barrowmoor is the ancient burial ground of the horselords of Nidal. Found in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains in what is known as the Atteran Ranches region of Nidal, the Barrowmoor is dotted with charcoal-encrusted mounds holding the remains of the first inhabitants of that land. Locals have a taboo about entering the Barrowmoor, believing that ancient curses will fall upon any trespassers. Some ignore such tales, lured by the prospect of discovering ancient, buried treasure. Those who return claim that the mounds cannot be accurately counted, and that new ones appear once every few years.
354523377:
Thorn River bandit camp

1 locations:
  • Tuskgutter's lair
366166527:
Prophet's Academy

Prophet's Academy is a vast manor house in the north-east of the Coins district of Absalom, purchased by Trevlin Crest and Ilrava Drogand, two followers of the Prophecies of Kalistrade who came to Absalom from Kerse to acquire the city's great riches. They obtain their wealth not through trade but through education, as it serves as an academy where their discipline is taught and connections are forged among the wealthy students who attend.
368640297:
Braganza

Braganza is a city on the Plains of Molthune on the western edge of that country, and is one of the centers of the worship of Abadar in the region.
379282907:
Tulaupan

Tulaupan is one of the three cities in Xopatl, along with Orjotoro and Ziplatna, that are together known as the Follower's Rests. Tulaupan is vital to Xopatl for trade with its neighbouring nation of Innazpa.
386637617:
Devils' Garden

Devil's Garden, also known as Cheliax's Garden, is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where both exiles from the nation and agents of House Thrune live together, enjoying wine, opera, and the company of each other most of which cannot return to their homeland. After the civil war, many nobles went to Absalom to escape persecution, although several were killed by the new regime, which did not provoke a strong response from Absalom beyond a letter of disapproval to their embassy. Tensions have eased in recent decades, and as long as these nobles do not act against Her Majestrix, they have nothing to fear. Throughout the district, hatred and detestance toward Ravounel can be observed.
388291667:
Pit of Raugsmauda

The Pit of Raugsmauda is located on Motaku Isle in the Shackles. It was the former subterranean lair of the infamous sorcerer Raugsmauda.

It was assumed that Raugsmauda was gone forever but, in 4512 AR, a group of adventurers descended into the Pit of Raugsmauda and discovered the sorcerer, now transformed into a vicious lich. The adventurers fought Raugsmauda, who fled westward and eventually settled on a small, uninhabited, cave-riddled volcanic isle that now has the name of Raugsmauda's Reach.

The upper levels of the Pit have long been plundered over the years, but much of the massive dungeons in the Pit's cavernous depths remain unexplored, and potentially hold Raugsmauda's treasures and horrors.
397292537:
Rolgrimmdur

Rolgrimmdur is built among the ruins of Grakodan by Khadon's engineers and is one of the most fortified dwarven cities in Five Kings Mountains. It is located about 50 miles southwest of the capital city of Highhelm.
412834297:
Dalun

Located in the south of the Irriseni province of Wintercrux on the Thundering River, Dalun is a small trade town that does business with the outside world.

Though Dalun strongly encourages trade with the outside world, which is essential for Irrisen's survival, its diplomatic efforts are ruined because of the Irriseni raids launched from Redtooth and Saarbotten in the Verge to the west. Indeed, Dalun is derisively called 'Gentle Dalun' by the people of Saarbotten despite the fact that Dalun supplies most of Saarbotten's food.

As of 4713 AR, Baroness Nadya was the ruler of Dalun. She had tried to foster stable trading relations with Southmoor and Varisia to little success. Dalun was responsible for supplying Redtooth and Saarbotten with grain and other foodstuffs. Nadya's bloodthirsty sister, Urgalaena, baroness of Saarbotten, often sabotaged Nadya's diplomatic attempts. Lady Nadya's chief steward, Betrona Harcatha, was actually a spy for her sister Urgalaena.
413603557:
Mavradia

Mavradia was once a great Iobarian city and capital of the breakaway realm of Zastel. Founded upon the ruins of a cyclopean settlement in The Caemorin in 1240 AR, nearly one third of the city was destroyed during the Drakeplague of 4519 AR, when dragons battled each other within the city's walls, convinced it held a cure. The city would later be attacked by giants from Fangard, and subsequently conquered by rebellious insurgents in 4607 AR.

Mavradia would finally collapse into ruin when the city was struck with a second plague that turned its survivors into "oozing deaths", mindless gelatinous creatures that attacked others. The settlement has remained abandoned since 4659 AR, left to the oozing deaths and dragons, both alive and undead.
422694577:
Sarain

Sarain is a small village in the River Kingdom of Pitax, close to the border with Mivon. Sarain is famous for its vineyards and, before the arrival of Castruccio Irovetti, the Liacenza family had overall control over most of the wine-making business of the area.
424989767:
Jezonna Oasis

Jezonna Oasis, or sometimes just Jezonna, is an oasis town located in the Meraz Desert of Qadira and is a rare source of both water and safety. It is headquarters to the Amellas al-Hekbah, an organization of desert giants granted imperial Kelesh citizenship as thanks for ensuring the safety of travellers crossing the Meraz Desert.

Jezonna Oasis has several encampments of desert giant tribes spread between the oasis' three lakes, which are known as the Three Wisdoms. The most prominent of these tribes is led by Janar Malakra, who also leads the Amellas al-Hekbah's mercenary organization.
433748317:
Isarn

The city of Isarn is the capital of Galt and home of its legislature, as well as of the Revolutionary Council.
437194847:
Occarin

Occarin is a village in the Carpenden Plains region of Andoran whose inhabitants are unknowingly and secretly almost all changeling children of three green hags.

472689997:
Davarn

The town of Davarn in the southwestern Five Kings Mountains is one of the centers of dwarven culture in the region.
480311727:
Egorian

Egorian (pronounced eh-GOR-ee-an) became the capital of Cheliax upon the Thrune Ascendancy in 4640 AR, replacing the city of Westcrown. House Thrune wished to create a new imperial capital to honor its new allegiances far from the nobles and history of Westcrown. It is also known as the City of Thorns.
521139667:
Valorfoe

The cathedral-like, Ustalavic fortress of Valorforge at the Gap of Valballus in the Hold of Belkzen was one of the fortresses of the defensive barrier of Sunwall. When the Sunwall fell in 4237 AR the veteran soldiers of Lastwall retreated to a second line of defense but the Ustalavs of Valorforge didn't abandon their fortress in the face of invincible orc hordes and all within were slaughtered. Now known as Valorfoe, the abandoned castle is shunned by the orcs due to the shadowy terrors that lurk within.
526325457:
Zetang

Zetang is capital of the empyrean-ruled Tian Xia nation of Tianjing. The Nyenreng Conclave rule from here, many of them too frail to leave the city. A port city on Point Aykishe along the nation's eastern coast, Zetang was victim of Zo pirate attacks when Lung Wa fell but has since been rebuilt. Rumors suggest the unhappy dead still persist among the shipwrecks in its harbor.
530056377:
Aroden's Redoubt

Aroden's Redoubt is a ruined fort that was the first line of defense against a possible invasion from Garund, located above the Arch of Aroden before the entrance to Corentyn. The fort is collapsing under its own weight, and historical tours no longer visit it.
539152207:
Brimstone Haruspex

Though highly independent, even proud orc chieftains sometimes seek advice. When this happens, most warlords send their seconds-in-command to the Endseers of the Brimstone Haruspex, a temple complex high in the caldera of the smoking Eye of the Unmaker stratovolcano within the northern Kodar Mountains of the Hold of Belkzen.
540750437:
Apertan

Apertan is a city in the Arcadian nation of Xopatl located to the south of the country's centre. Apertan is home to the largest market in all Xopatl that attracts merchants and traders from across the land. The market itself is an enormous open-air tower with many levels, each teaming with shops and stalls. Visitors use a system of platforms to raise and lower themselves between the various stalls and shops.
550035597:
Thousand Relics Pass

Thousand Relics Pass is a mountain pass through the northern World's Edge Mountains of Taldor. It is bordered to the west by the Falling River and to the east by Aroden's Eye.
551733287:
Whitewillow

The Whitewillow was a swamp in the Shimmerglens of shallow water with beautiful trees and lovely flowers cared for by Myriana, but when she was killed and tormented with the death and resurrection as an undead of her lover, the trees turned black and its waters became fetid and twisted.
558668057:
Vannarak

Vannarak is one of the many cities of the naga empire of Nagajor, and is located in its far western reaches on the nation's northernmost island. A relatively new city, it is built around and within the remains of a massive beached kaiju; its residents mine valuable materials from the beast's remains, and academics and wizards travel to study still-unexplored parts of the corpse.
565733987:
Maecho

Maecho is a city in Hwanggot, a nation in Tian Xia. In contrast to Haseong, Maecho is a slower-paced city, making it a relaxing destination. The city's many hot springs are its largest attraction.
567232297:
Mudpot

The Mudpot is a region of Nirmathas near the source of the Deepcut River in the Mindspin Mountains. Characterized by the large hot springs that bubble throughout it, the Mudpot is largely avoided by humanoids due to the population of oozes and elementals that reside here. Those that do brave the Mudpot are attracted by the rare alchemical agents and gemstones that are created by the volcanic activity in the region.
568933327:
Low Isle

Low Isle, along with its sister city of High Isle, provides much of the seafood for the nation of Xopatl. Located along the south-western coast of Xopatl on a small island in Lake Tapipallati, Low Isle is renowned for its seafood. The fishermen of Low Isle tend to remain nearer the coastline and fish for giant crabs, freshwater eels, and various other sea creatures.
585535317:
Seaward Fort

The Seaward Fort is built on one of the two small islands of Corentyn's harbor, known as the Navy Yard district. It serves as the head of naval command.

The Seaward Fort is one of the nine famous forts of Corentyn, according to the classical listing.
589241917:
Sakakabe

Sakakabe is a busy coastal town in Minkai. It is built on a favourable landing on an otherwise treacherous coastline.

Sakakabe is constructed on a series of steep, concentric terraces that extend from the port to the top of the hills, where the richest houses are built, while the lowest terraces lean on the ruins of a stone fortress built millennia ago. The ancient stone walls are adorned with reliefs of ancient warriors, but these images are now almost completely hidden by modern structures.

The town is known for its entertainment district (or hanamachi), which is built on one of the highest terraces, centred on a large street called the Way of the Songbird.
590857957:
House of Lords

The House of Lords is a municipal building of Magnimar's city government in Varisia. Located in the wealthy Alabaster District, the temple-like structure holds offices for each of the city's seven great aristocratic families. Members of the public can appeal to the representatives of each of these families here in order to air grievances, ask for favors, or otherwise deal with the needs of the citizens at large. Magnimar's lesser aristocratic families do not have permanent offices here, but may rent them for a small fee should they so choose.
596911147:
Chillblight

Chillblight is the regional capital of Feyfrost, the Irrisen province ruled by the cold fey. The city is located in northeastern Irrisen on the Frozen Road, and was built by the cold fey themselves. Being creatures of the First World, they eschewed traditional building materials, and carved the whole city out of ice that shimmers in the cold, northern sun.
599087717:
Little Lirgen

Little Lirgen is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where the diaspora of the fallen nation of Lirgen gathers, and most of them maintain their nation's astronomical traditions. Mosaics of stars fill the streets, painted like the night sky, and although in the first few years after the Eye of Abendego opened they hoped the storm would dissipate, they are increasingly uncertain about whether to remain culturally separate from the rest of Absalom or assimilate. The death of prophecy has rendered many of their stargazing rituals and artifacts obsolete, and a feeling of emptiness fills the hearts of these residents. The neighborhood is also where the city's lizardfolk population gathers, with inns offering live fish to eat, hot showers to sleep in, and lights that provide solar warmth in the winter.
605722937:
Jormurdun

Jormurdun is one of the ten ancient Sky Citadels built by the newly emergent dwarves at the end of their Quest for Sky. Lost from history for thousands of years after invasion and disaster until 4713 AR, it is located in the Wolfcrags, a largely uninhabited range of hills in northwestern parts of Sarkoris's (formerly the Worldwound's) Frostmire region.
606202607:
Olivine Tomb of Djederet II

Little is known about the Olivine Tomb of Djederet II, located in Osirion during its First Age in what is now Thuvia. Its namesake pharaoh Djederet II was the nephew of Hakotep I and succeeded him as Osirion's ruler in -1611 AR, and is known for reverting many of the Sky Pharaoh's works.
606761247:
Hidden Lake

Hidden Lake is a hidden city of alchemists that is concealed within the depths of Forest of Thieves in the north of central Xopatl. Hidden Lake remains hidden thanks to the magical properties of the Forest of Thieves. Those who venture into the forest with a destination in mind re-emerge mere hours later; only those who can clear their mind and embrace getting lost can ever find their way to Hidden Lake. The city itself is home to many alchemists who use the magical plants of the Forest of Thieves to create particularly potent concoctions.
609832307:
Fort Machema

Fort Machema is an abandoned fort a few days' journey from Tamran along the shore of Lake Encarthan. The fort is in a well-protected area overlooking the lake, but there are no soldiers to maintain it.
613468467:
Cynosure Tower

10 locations:
  • Aquaretum
  • Bent Rib Alley
  • Burn
  • Embassy Building
  • Pig
  • Pug's Contraptions
  • Rose and Rake Theater
  • Seven's Sawmill
  • Varisian Council
  • Washers' Row
632066387:
Pit of Zedoran

The Pit of Zedoran is a vast sinkhole within the depths of the Northern Fangwood. Streams of water carrying leaves and organic debris cascade down its sides, and layers of moss and lichen cover hanging vines and ancient roots. The thick forest canopy allows little sunlight to reach the moss-covered earth.

The pit is named for the green dragon Zedoran who as of 4722 AR still laired here. At the edge of the central pit is a wooden cage lift, manually operated by Zedoran's minions. There are two cages, one at the top and the other at the bottom of the pit, each large enough to hold eight humans.
634985247:
Westgate Bathhouse

The Westgate Bathhouse is one of the most popular establishments of its kind in the city, receiving visitors day and night who come to enjoy its spacious pools. The marble structure gives it a cathedral-like feel due to its size, and it offers massage services, a market, and private pools for couples or those plotting plans and deals.
643390227:
Segada

Segada is a city of Mahwek Arcadians that serves as a gateway to Degasi and other reaches of Arcadia's interior. Its economic importance, already significant as a hub for Arcadian commerce, has grown from recent trade with Avistan.
651235007:
Postern

The Postern Gate, or simply the Postern, is the eastern gate of Absalom. It is the smallest of the city's gates, only 50 feet high and 40 feet wide, and the only one that is normally kept closed and locked. It is a defensive building designed by Aroden and made of thick stone with balconies, and is used mainly to get goods to and from the Starwatch Keep.

The gate was often the focus of siege attacks, and was breached during the Siege of Prophets even if the enemy forces proved unable to penetrate the city. A carved stone set in the last hall within the Postern commemorates the deepest point the enemy reached. The Postern Gate also serves as the headquarters of Eastgate's district guard, the Post Guard.
657079967:
Kerad

Kerad is a small peasant community on the Rimeflow River in Irrisen's Bleakmarch province. It has little to recommend it, and is often referred to as "Poor Little Kerad" by locals.

Between the sprawling Morozny to the east and the Zelen to the west, Kerad has little to offer to the merchants and travelers of the Rimeflow River. A simple inn and a small drinking house are its only attractions. No Jadwiga has been sent to govern the Poor Little Kerad and the townsfolk, mostly fur trappers and fishermen, are happy for this. Kerad's trappers sail down the river to sell their goods in Zelen. The town elects its mayor every ten years and the current mayor is Anichka Labacova.

Anichka and a number of the townsfolk are in fact members of the underground resistance organization called Heralds of Summer's Return and meetings of the Heralds are not uncommon in Kerad. A carefully hidden shrine of Desna is tended by the priest Dusana Rivanov. Fugitives from the White Witches or other arms of Irrisen's law often find shelter in Kerad.
659088667:
Cave of Darkfear

The Cave of Darkfear is actually an abandoned trapper's shack in Brinestump Marsh. Licktoad goblins used to challenge each other to see if they could go inside, because although there was no danger, the atmosphere was spooky for the little goblins.
659999927:
Castle of the Green Feather

The Castle of the Green Feather was the flying castle of the knights of the Order of the Green Feather. It could easily transport legions across long distances. The castle is thought to have survived Earthfall, but shortly thereafter the arcane magic keeping it aloft failed, and the fortress crash-landed on the Kodar Mountains near the modern-day northern border of Varisia. Some of the knights of the Order of the Green Feather were turned into graveknights and liches upon death, and they continue to guard their sacred fortress and serve the Peacock Spirit today.
664553077:
Albatross

The small, coastal fishing town of Albatross perches precariously on a cliffside off the coast of Nidal on Conqueror's Bay. Although it is a settlement of less than 400 souls, it is home to many times that number of its namesake avians. Residents of Albatross claim to be able to predict the weather, foretell ill omens, and see the approach of death simply by observing and interpreting the flights of these birds.
679922637:
Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall

1 locations:
  • Palace Ormuz
680790047:
Kibwe

Kibwe is a large, walled city-state of trade and mining with a diverse population of Mwangi peoples and non-humans, situated in the eastern Mwangi Jungle west of the Ndele Gap.
681050377:
Volfast's Tower

High in Volfast's Tower stands the undead Volfast Raven-Banner, cursed to forever remain at his post after he drunkenly boasted to Queen Sascha that, if not for his limited mortal life, he would eternally hold back the Frozen Fog itself in the queen's name. Unfortunately for him, she granted his request, and transformed him into his current state. Here he remains until someone can defeat him in single combat. Some say that whoever defeats him would not only free him, but also inherit his cursed post, while others say they would gain a great, hidden wealth somewhere in the tower.
696837557:
Crow

The Crow is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. Standing 220 feet high, there are no known entrances into the piling.
697345687:
Katapesh

702605097:
Helkgen

Helkgen is a small town on the shores of Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Bleakmarch.

The Ulfen residents of Helkgen are struggling to support the merchant ships hugging the shores of Glacier Lake as Jadwiga overlords demand, but also to collect sufficient tribute throughout the year to give to the unforgiving scrags of Scrag Deep.

As of 4713 AR, Helkgen's mayor was Valka Dagsdottir, who earned the ire of townsfolk while working hard to keep them safe from the scrags.
708584047:
Blue Tower

5 locations:
  • Antler Lodge
  • Gilltown

  • Wondervale
712635287:
Vale of Shadows

The Vale of Shadows is a dangerous valley located in the Five Kings Mountains, in which one of Zon-Kuthon's Star Towers is located. Here the planar fabric between the Netherworld and Golarion is weak, and the Vale all but appears to be within the Netherworld itself, and creatures and monsters from that plane live within. The elves of Elistia forbid entrance to the Vale of Shadows, believing nothing good comes from it and knowing few who venture inside return alive.
749943147:
Absalom Mint

The Absalom Mint, located near Azlanti Keep in Absalom's Wise Quarter that produces much of the city's coinage—a function that, due to the City at the Center of the World's outsized influence on trade and commerce, literally and figuratively shapes money across the Inner Sea region and beyond. Coins minted in Absalom are either accepted in locations as distant as Tian Xia and Vudra, or is the standard by which nations measure and mint their own currency.

The Mint re-mints currencies from other nations, often increasing the resulting coins' purity in the process. Its building also houses the city's Office of Taxation.
773614507:
Yin-Sichasi

The underground city of Yin-Sichasi beneath the Gossamer Mountains is home to the witch-queen Lady Lang Loi, who rules over the monster-dominated brigand nation of Shenmen in Tian Xia. Most people who live in Yin-Sichasi are the witch-queen's slaves.

Many of the inhabitants are jorogumo, like the ruler herself, and on rare occasions will turn into undead known colloquially as "bone-fang jorogumo". These creatures occupy a half-ostracized, half-exalted position as occult advisors and spiritual intermediaries within the city.
783554507:
Baslwief

The mining village of Baslwief is located in the foothills of the eastern Fenwall Mountains in Varisia, on the banks of the Sarwin River.
786559517:
Fort Bandu

Fort Bandu is a fortification in eastern Vidrian, located in the Bandu Hills on the banks of the River of Lost Tears near the border with the Mwangi Expanse.
787071037:
Haeringar's Keep

Haeringar's Keep is the massive fortress of Haeringar the Everlasting. It is located in the Irriseni province of Feyfrost just south of the edge of the Winterwall Glacier. The fortress has enormous walls and 18 ice-encrusted stone towers which are patrolled by ice elementals. Haeringar's Keep was built there long before Baba Yaga's Winter War. Haeringar is an ancient fiendish frost giant oracle who is revered by the frost giants of Irrisen and Winterwall Glacier. Even the great Jarl Grungginnir of Holvirgang presents himself to him for counsel and blessings. Haeringar eats human flesh and is attended by a harem of ogresses. The gates of the fortress are always open but all the past adventurers, who have entered the keep trying to steal Haeringar's vast treasure, have failed.
798241117:
Sumitha

Sumitha is an ancient Ghol-Gani ruin on the Island of Empty Eyes in the Shackles.
799464397:
Battle of the Burning Child

The Battle of the Burning Child was fought in 4237 AR on the eastern plains of the Hold of Belkzen between militia from Lastwall and advancing orc hordes. Here the demoralized and routed forces of Lastwall made a stand when they spotted a screaming human peasant child shrouded in flames in the middle of the orc ranks. The orcs were just as confused by its appearance, but the sight of the child caused the Lastwall soldiers to hold their ground and temporarily drive back the nonhuman invaders. When it was over, the battlefield was searched, but no remains of the child could be found. To this day, travelers who venture nearby on moonless nights claim that they can hear a child crying.
799856317:
Kintargo

812258727:
Bokken's Hut

Bokken's Hut is located within the Rostland Hinterlands region of the Stolen Lands, a few miles south of Brevoy's border. The hut functions as both the residence and potion shop of an eccentric alchemist named Bokken, housing a modest alchemical laboratory.
843677627:
Delvehaven

Delvehaven is the Pathfinder lodge in the old Chelaxian capital of Westcrown. It was founded in the late 47th century AR by Venture-Captain Aiger Ghaelfin.
845261217:
Archheathen's Garrison

Archheathen's Garrison is a cluster of offices and civil administration buildings located within this fortress in Corentyn. The basements of the buildings contain secret tunnels leading to safe houses in the eastern part of the Noble Quarter.
845607147:
Fort Holiday

Fort Holiday is a refuge built by the marooned survivors of Raptor Island in the Shackles. It is located atop a rocky hill of the island, at the fork of the Broken Hope River and its main tributary. It consists of a number of huts surrounded by a wooden palisade.

The castaways continue to strengthen the palisade of their small refuge with stones and welcome any new survivors who can make it to the gates of Fort Holiday.
847703827:
Foreign Quarter Council Hall

The Foreign Quarter Council Hall is where the district's council meets, and Diplomatic Minister Ferridan Severus also maintains several offices there that are assigned to foreign diplomats for their meetings or other tasks when within the city's grounds.
848675507:
Miratanza

Miratanza, also known as the Floating Market, is located in the Rego Laina in Westcrown. It is the only area open to transactions and commerce on Westcrown Island or the Parego Regicona. It allows servants for the inhabitants of Regicona to shop for goods without visiting the mainland. Vendors can rent space or simply sell from ships tied up at the market.
880810497:
Madinani Manor

The Madinani Manor is an imposing estate with a white wall, gardens with plants used for arcane or alchemical purposes, and a three-story building in the center. This impressive structure serves as the home of Lady Darchana, one of Absalom's most prominent political figures and a master of arcane magic.
890823697:
Massacre House

The Massacre House is the name given to the stone building that serves as a monastery for the Sisters of Eiseth, though no one dares call it that to one of them. Those who fail to show sufficient piety or respect to House Thrune are taken to the monastery, where the sisters deal with the disloyalty of the citizens.
893343377:
Deadeye's Haunt

Deadeye's Haunt is a narrow valley on the border between Molthune and Nirmathas, at the southern edge of Fangwood. It is the site of the bloodiest battle in the war between the two nations.
902789697:
Lightning Stones

According to ancient Garundi legend, the Lightning Stones mark a battleground between earth and air elementals. In fact, this line of twenty-foot-tall menhirs only attracts these elementals, and when they encounter each other, old grudges ensure it does not end peaceably. The menhirs run through the southern reaches of the Katapesh section of the Barrier Wall mountains near the border with Nex. True to their name, the Lightning Stones attract lightning from the storms that are common to the area. The stones glow when struck by lightning, revealing runes and glyphs otherwise invisible to observers. This written language is unique but shares characteristics with the languages of ancient Katapesh and Nex, and the text appears to describe how to acquire and use power for the betterment of everyone.
902995547:
Asmodeus Mirage

The Asmodeus Mirage is (or was, see below) a demiplane created by the destruction of an unnamed herald of Asmodeus by Rovagug. A connection between it and Golarion appears once every 100 years in the deserts of northwestern Katapesh, hard against the Brazen Peaks and north-northwest of the source of the Pale River. This connection remains open for a single 24-hour period before disappearing again for a century.
905557907:
Mezdrubal

Mezdrubal is a small town at the southwestern region of Devil's Arches island in the Shackles. It is ruled by a petty buccaneer called Longbeard who resents his tribute that Hell Harbor demands at the beginning of each month.

Longbeard could not even think to refuse to pay his tribute, and he usually takes out his frustration at the poor victims of his cruel and often arbitrary judgement. Mezdrubal's ruler is also known for his love of fine clothing and jewelry, and many pirate vessels head to his town to sell such wares to Longbeard.
907939767:
Skull's Crossing

Skull's Crossing is the name of the massive arched dam that holds back the waters of the Storval Deep, releasing a steady stream into the Skull River. Located in central Varisia it is an ancient ruin from the Thassilonian era over 10,000 years ago.
919418347:
Eastgate Hall

Eastgate Hall is an ornate stone structure that once served a group of adventurers fighting against the corruption spread by Rovagug's followers. Today, it is used by the Eastgate Council, which meets monthly to resolve issues arising in the district.
924278487:
Ruwido

Ruwido is a hovel-village secreted away in the notorious Ustalavic county of Virlych where misbegotten, cast-out, mad, and deformed souls can find a home, hide their faces from the world, and find a modicum of peace and acceptance. The village is located in the western hills of the Ghorcha Pass close to the waters of the Barrow Run. The settlement is shunned by practically everyone, including patrols from Lastwall and Amaans, while the villagers themselves are suspicious of any strangers approaching. One of the few regular and trusted visitors to Ruwido is Azra, a mute cleric of Desna.
924533957:
Gray Observatory

The Gray Observatory is a public venue where astronomers from across the nation and abroad gather to discuss their stargazing findings. The building sits atop a high hill, away from the nighttime light pollution.
932466567:
Summerglen

Summerglen is a deep-sided canyon in the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach whose walls are studded with crystals that seem to capture the warmth of the sun, increasing the canyon's temperature.
939285837:
College of Dimensional Studies

One of the many magical fraternities that make their home in the city of Katapesh, the College of Dimensional Studies specializes in magic relating to interdimensional travel or visiting truly distant parts of the Universe. The domed building of the college is located in the crowded Inner City district of Katapesh and contains not only the normal dormitories and laboratories expected of a wizards' college, but also a huge telescope open even for the general public's use on some nights. The College of Dimensional Studies is also, therefore, one of the foremost schools of astronomy in the Inner Sea region.

One of the most unique features of the college is its lack of doors. All students are expected to possess an array of travel magic and are expected to be able to teleport between rooms. Students of the college are also expected to create scrolls of transport magic as part of their payment for the college's unique education. The College of Dimensional Studies is also one of the few institutions on Golarion to house one of the rare and mysterious astralabes, devices of enormous utility to those seeking to explore the planes of the Great Beyond.
953921207:
Wyrmwatch

1006673907:
Veil of the Spire

The Yolubilis River begins high on the slopes of Mount Spire, just below the tree line, as a trickle that turns into a series of waterfalls. These waterfalls gather multiple streams as they surge downward, and the tallest, called the Veil of the Spire, falls more than 300 feet into a large pool that is the river's highest navigable point. Behind the waterfalls is an extensive networks of caves, some of which contain their own streams and falls. Many of these caves are riddled with cave fishers, chardas, and other monsters. In addition, several tribes of goblins and kobolds compete for a living in these caverns.
1023828647:
Sphinx Gate

The Sphinx Gate is located in the northwest of Absalom and is usually open even at night due to the constant traffic in the area. It connects to the farms of the region and is used by the First Guard to patrol the Cairnlands, taking its name from the enormous sphinx statue erected there. During the Unraveling Siege, when the proteans attacked the city, dozens of riddles appeared at the gate, and after a guard solved one, the proteans were no longer able to dominate the city's skies, proving instrumental in the victory against them. Only those who have aided Absalom on countless occasions have access to the riddles, as no one knows what dangerous magic they may contain, and of all the city's entrances, this is the only one that has never been breached, partly due to its proximity to the Azlanti Keep.
1038710287:
Lackthroat

The walled town of Lackthroat is located near the source of the Egelsee River in northern Numeria, hard against the Estrovian Forest of the crusader nation of Mendev. Known as a haven for ne'er-do-wells and mercenaries, the citizens of Lackthroat take pride in indulging their own vices and those of anyone passing through. Despite, or perhaps because, of this, Lackthroat has served as the staging ground for many legends among mercenaries and other adventurers from Numeria and beyond.
1040606567:
Great Temple of Lissala

The Great Temple of Lissala, located in central Bakrakhan, was the largest and most famous temple of Lissala, the patron goddess of Thassilon, and home to the Chalice of Lissala, an artifact gifted by the goddess herself. The temple sank beneath the Varisian Gulf when Earthfall hit, and it is now home to Vanush, a kraken who uses the Chalice of Lissala to lure sailors to their dooms. Vanush seeks to gather sacrifices and relics so the kraken can restore Lissala's faith in the Inner Sea region, with itself as leader.
1042588197:
Greengold

4 locations:

1048510637:
House of the Moon

The House of the Moon is an ancient Desnan shrine located on the Storval Plateau of Varisia. It is frequently visited by the Lyrune-Quah tribe of the Shoanti, and is within their territory.
1072499007:
Almas

1 locations:

1076413747:
Tidewater Rock

Tidewater Rock is a fortified tower over 70 feet tall built on the rocky shore of Windward Isle. It is made of sturdy masonry walls atop a 10-foot-high stone base, filled with packed dirt. A stone stair rises to the heavy front door. Tidewater Rock sits right on the lagoon's edge and commands a good view of almost the entire island. At high tide, the water rises almost to the height of its stone base. The current owner of the Rock is Agasta Smythee, widow of the pirate Iron Bert Smythee.
1077937227:
Avennara

Avennara is an elf village located near the western edge of the Fierani Forest halfway between the settlements of Riverspire and Erages. An elf gate within the village opens to a large number of other gates around Golarion. This gate has been activated thanks to the Godsrain connecting it to unknown locations, with strange animals that have come though causing havoc and bounties for their capture have been place on them.

The Lantern Bearers, an elven secret society that hunts the drow, use a site close to the gate as their main meeting place.
1096469867:
Weeping Grandfather

The Weeping Grandfather is a volcanic peak in the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. It is best known as the location for the Cave of Zero Incantation—a now abandoned site sacred to the Muted God.
1124036307:
Peddlegate

The city of Peddlegate is located in the northeast of the nation of Druma, hard against the Five Kings Mountains.
1128012567:
Iron Rose

The Iron Rose is an opulent palace located in a verdant valley in the Menador Mountains of northeastern Isger, close to the border with Druma. Its mistress is Lady Kaltessa Iyis, the high priestess of the archdevil Mammon in the Inner Sea region. From here she directs her centuries-long plots on behalf of her infernal master.

Blackjacket mercenaries guard the palace and protect the valley against attacks from Molthune to the north being situated in one of the few passages that directly connects the two nations.

1156342957:
Withrun House

The Withrun House is a school founded by Kalistocrats who instill their doctrine in the children of Absalom's wealthiest families, who are unaware of the cult's control. Due to its similarity to the Prophet's Academy, it maintains a rivalry with them. They also have their own team of ruk called the Withrun Wraiths.
1161169647:
Briar Henge

Briar Henge is a druidic gathering place in the Verduran Forest centered around a circle of magical menhirs. Many centuries ago, druids created a base of briar walls using magic around the structure, and over time, different druidic groups have used it as their base.

1174798507:
Mobhad Leigh

From the Shoanti term meaning "steps into hell," this perfectly circular pit is located at the base of the Kodar Mountains, on the Storval Plateau of northern Varisia, approximately 25 miles northeast of the source of the Aloren River. A set of spiral stairs run around the edge of the hole, but ends after only a few hundred feet. Beyond this point it is necessary to climb the sheer walls or fly. Few people know how deep the Mobhad Leigh actually is, although some rumor that it is so deep that it bypasses the Darklands region of Nar-Voth completely and opens directly into Sekamina.
1189346447:
Sable Barrow

Sable Barrow is a hill that overlooks the Numerian city of Chesed. The Pathfinder Gojan the Sharp observed a golden statue appear on the Barrow the night before a seemingly inevitable slave revolt. The statue hefted a mighty hammer and seemed to be threatening the city with it, and carried the mark of the god Abadar on his chest armor. Gojan believed that the statue's presence prevented the slave revolt, and it disappeared four days later. Most agree that the golden statue was actually the Lawgiver, the herald of Abadar.

Lawgiver's appearance instead inspired the slaves to join with the city's defenders in order to repel an unprecedented, and since unequaled, assault upon the city by barbarians. After the city's successful defense against the raid, Chesed abolished its slave trade, emancipated its slaves, and decreed that any slave brought into the city would be declared free within an hour of arrival if not registered with the city's government.
1190581107:
Desgard's Thousand Columns

Desgard's Thousand Columns is an ancient Thassilonian ruin in eastern Varisia. It is a field featuring a large number of towered pillars and pylons of jagged crystal. They were used by the runelords of Sloth, particularly Krune, for conjuring armies of extraplanar forces to defend the realm of Haruka. They were activated by secret runes given to Krune by Lissala.
1197416167:
Pek Peh

The town of Pek Peh is one of the settlements scattered throughout the desolate, monster-haunted land of Shenmen. Ruled over by the fearsome jorogumo, monstrous hybrids of humans and spiders, Pek Peh is forced to resort to banditry and kidnapping to keep up with the jorogumo's demands for sentient sacrificial victims.
1205774747:
Versade Villa

3 locations:

  • Scarnetti Villa
  • Valdemar Villa
1224301547:
Bazaar of Sails

2 locations:

  • Carent's Camp
1247921037:
Hemachandra

The Hemachandra, also known as the "Seat of the Golden Moon", is a legendary adventuring site in the eastern Divine Garden region of Vudra. The terraced tower contains a shrine to each of the thousands of deities of the Vudran pantheon, from hundreds of minor gods on its lowest level to Irori alone on its highest level nearly a day's climb upward. Acolytes and priests live in some of the tower's middle levels.
1261694777:
Kutaban

Kutaban (pronounced ku-ta-BAAN) is a mercantile free city of the Valashmai Jungle located on the southern river border of Nagajor. Warded by the ancient rib cage of a deceased kaiju, the city is considered the gateway into and out of the Valashmai Jungle. Adventurers, travelers, and goods leaving or entering Valashmai all move through Kutaban, enriching the local merchant lords. Though they typically feud with each other, these merchant lords unite to protect this status whenever an upstart trade city tries to compete with Kutaban for its monopoly on Valashmai's northern border.
1266210647:
Imperial Marina

The Imperial Marina was established in 4634 AR in the easternmost part of the Westcrown mainland to gain naval superiority over river trade. In 4716 AR, it was used by the Glorious Reclamation as a prison for the nobility who opposed their rule.
1269265487:
Queen Bes

Queen Bes is the only settlement of any size on the Shackles island of Besmara's Throne. Because the entire isle is a holy site to the worshipers of the pirate goddess Besmara, the town contains a large proportion of pilgrims.
1276015077:
Lion Sleeps Inn

The Lion Sleeps Inn is a traveller's stop 35 miles north-east of the Taldan settlement of Zimar.
1292417677:
Kar-Azakh

Kar-Azakh is a town located in a cavern of the Five Kings Mountains that surrounds a thunderous 200-foot-tall waterfall called the Cascade. It is located in the southern Five Kings Range halfway between Taggoret and Kykar.

The falling water's roar and cavern's echoes drown out most attempts at verbal communication, and most residents wear hearing protection outside of their homes and use sign language to communicate. The waterfall also turns dozens of waterwheels that power its many mills.
1294132827:
Orjotoro

1 locations:
  • Valley of the Chosen Children
1298449227:
Fort Nunder

Fort Nunder is the Chernasardo Rangers' stronghold closest to the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas. It sits against the west side of a steep hill called Nunder Scarp. Decades ago, the Rangers constructed a vault beneath the hill to secure specialized weapons and equipment. The two best trapsmiths among the Rangers, Dargrit Foehewer and Ekaterin Fletcher, built two separate, trapped passages leading to the vault. Fort Nunder is more of a storehouse than a fortress. A handful of barracks are available, but the bulk of the fort is dedicated to storage space.
1309955407:
Reanpharos

Reanpharos, or Reanphoros, also called the City Between Two Rivers, is the capital of the city-state of Pol-Reanpharos in the Iblydan archipelago. It is home of Chinostes, the Fallen Blade, an Iblydan hero-god of tragedy and sacrifice.
1323650277:
Castle Korvosa

Easily the most imposing structure in a city of monumental architecture, Castle Korvosa is the fortress-citadel home of the monarchs Korvosa. The castle itself is built on an ancient four-sided, flat-topped pyramid known as the Grand Mastaba, built millennia ago by the Shoanti people. Built on the mainland's Citadel Hill, it can be seen from anywhere in the city.
1330581707:
Liachora

Liachora was the capital of the Iblydan archipelago city-state of Pol-Liachora that also included the city of Aelyosos. It was destroyed by the thalassic behemoth Ousmariku at the beginning of the Age of Lost Omens, in a battle during which many Iblydan hero-gods were killed. Its citizens were either killed in the attack or fled to Aelyosos, which became the city-state's inheritor-capital. The ruins of Liachora have since been claimed by sedacthys.
1339316317:
El-Amara

The ruins of el-Amara are in western Osirion, close to the border with Thuvia. The ruins lie close to where the Junira River and the Scarab River meet.

On the other side of the Junira, in nearby Thuvia, are the Ruins of Akhenaten.

El-Amara was once the provincial capital of Thuvia, when Thuvia was still part of the Ancient Osirion. It was abandoned and fell to ruin when pharaohs started to ignore their distant vassal state. It was in el-Amara that High Theurgist Fentet-Pesu, the last Osirian governor of Thuvia, was assassinated in -841 AR. Since its fall, few had visited the half-buried spires and crumbling bell towers of el-Amara, until a few centuries ago when disciples of Ahriman took to the ruins to establish a base near Osirion. As of 4712 AR, el-Amara was populated by sepid divs led by general Karesh-Ekhial and hordes of ghul and edimmu thralls.
1344067877:
Lady's Light

7 locations:
  • Battlefield
  • Cavern of Many Mouths
  • Maroux's Isle
  • Solifugid Nest
  • Temple of Gogunta
  • Troglodyte Caves
  • Wreck of the Impdrake
1352679587:
Rovagug's Hall

Rovagug's Hall is a ruin that is most known for being raided by a group that took their name after their exploits here, the Raiders of Rovagug's Hall, and is located in the southwest section of the Scrape region of the Isle of Kortos.
1358187217:
Saad

Saad is a small town in the River Kingdoms region controlled by Daggermark, and home to Daggermark's cavalry.

The hamelt's two dozen large timber buildings are protected by a motte-and-bailey wall, with several tall timber towers that overlook the surrounding plains. Outside of the wall are many well-guarded horse farms. All of these war-horses are the interbred stock of Yenchabur stallions brought to the area in 4402 AR.

Saad is also the main trading center for the merchant traffic coming from Tymon, and is often the target of bandits who plague all trade routes across the region.
1369174727:
Westcrown

  • Korradath
  • Taranik House
  • Vaneo Arvanxi

  • Arodennama
  • and 44 others…
1369793797:
Dragon-Eel Port

Located near where the Silkstring River meets the Sea of Eels, the Hu clan's enclave of Dragon-Eel Port coordinates Songbai's maritime trade over the inland sea. The prices of the nation's commodities are discussed here between representatives of the Songbai Shogunate, the Four Notables and, most recently, advisors from Goka.
1371077077:
Chilémbi

Chilémbi is a town in northern Xopatl, located near the source of the Follower's River and the Traveler's River. Its residents spend weeks following the large lizards, called tunbaros, that roam the nearby grasslands. They await an opportunity to capture and tame one so they might sell them as mounts, as they are well-known across Xopatl as the best steeds in the country.
1374352437:
Bieralei

Bieralei is a fishing village located on the Kyonin River. The village is famous for its monthly fishing festivals at the time of the full moon, where locals use the occasion to show off their largest and most impressive catches. A crayfish called the Kavakex lives in the swamp's waters and is respected for its role in cleaning the waters of pollution.
1378594377:
Absalom

  • Shoreline
  • Dawnfoot

  • Blue Tower
  • Beldrin's Tower
