  • and 237 others…
1384214947:
Pickled Imp

The Pickled Imp is a curio shop in The Docks run by the infamous Guaril Karela, though he's rarely seen there. It now operates more like a pawn shop, keeping no records of any transactions, making it impossible to know what's being sold, bought, or who the customers are. Captain Elmoira Taggart used to work as a clerk there, and when anyone tries to suggest the wares aren't entirely legal, the employees point out that she still frequents the shop and there have never been any investigations into the establishment.
1409687677:
Ranulf's Keep

Ranulf's Keep was built by a member of the Ulfen Guard of the same name who protected his emperor until the latter died of natural causes. He was allowed to take whatever he could from the treasury and went north where he built his keep in the Tusk Mountains. He didn't realize the place was a dragon breeding ground, and after fighting an epic battle against one of these beasts, he defended the site for three decades and ordered an avalanche to be triggered to bury the keep after his death. Over the years, changing temperatures have allowed the entrance to one of its towers to remain open, although most of the structure is under snow, guarded by the kellid tribes of the region who followed this brave man.
1414151327:
Summer Grove

Summer Grove, in Nidal's southern Uskwood, is reachable solely in midsummer and absent the rest of the year. This hidden glade exudes beauty and tranquility. Wildflowers and lush grasses thrive beneath fragrant linden and sweet cherry trees. The albino Shades of the Uskwood druids and their spiders have never disrupted this place.
1433309627:
Sanqatada Cinqarda

The Sanqatada Cinqarda is an immense cathedral dedicated to the five founders of Westcrown, which predates Cheliax and Andoran as provinces of the empire and remains shining, blocking any attempt to deface the facade in any way. The 30-foot-tall limestone statues beside each doorway represent one of the founders: Dotara, Palmor, Rixana, Adel, and Crucisal.
1437152917:
City Mortuary

The City Mortuary in Lubbertown is next to the Burying Ground on the outskirts of town. It is a chapel jointly run by the churches of Cayden Cailean, Calistria, and Besmara. Those who can afford it are buried in niches within the building.
1438601427:
Mewru

Mewru (pronounced miu-ROO) is a city-state of outcasts, criminals, and scavengers of the underwater nation of Xidao, located in the Xidao Gulf of eastern Tian Xia. It is located in Western Xidao, the part west of the Aya-Maru trench.
1455135597:
Tomb of Statues

The Tomb of Statues is the burial place of Pharaoh An-Hepsu III's family and servants, located on the western banks of Osirion's River Sphinx north of the city of An. Unlike in similar tombs, where the family and retainers of a pharaoh were buried alive or put to death following the monarch's passing, An-Hepsu III took advantage of his medusa consort's power, and had them all turned to stone.
1465934927:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse for the Foreign Quarter is a stone building where its judges are renowned for their knowledge of foreign law, and most crimes are tried in its jurisdiction because they are committed by foreigners. The Sleepless Suns maintain a presence in the building, assisting as bailiffs when needed.
1467996897:
Citadel of the Weary Sky

The Citadel of the Weary Sky, formerly known as the Jaizun Citadel, is a mysterious and enigmatic historical site located in the realm of Rahadoum. It holds a significant place in the history of the Jistka Imperium, a once-powerful empire known for its mastery of elemental magic and extraplanar conjurations.
1479088727:
Spider's Nest

The Spider's Nest is crater several miles in diameter located as of 4713 AR in Irrisen. It formed in 4529 AR when a gigantic explosion shook the eastern Hoarwood Forest, and out poured plumes of smoke, fire, and giant spiders. Queen Yelizaveta sent her armies to defeat this sudden spider horde before it could spread much beyond the forest, and they succeeded. Few have explored the area since, and no one yet knows the cause of the original explosion.
1484233357:
Orlendas

Orlendas has been destroyed and rebuilt over a dozen times in its history. Plagues, fires, wars, and centaur hordes have all been the cause of Orlendas' fall at least once, but the town's importance to Orlov and Franax as a source of timber for their shipbuilding industries has kept settlers returning. The demand for lumber has waned over the years, but, along with small pelts, it still remains one of the town's primary imports.

The town's current komar, Galtan ranger Kasrel Unilich, has brought investment to the town, and Orlendas is now better defended and more prosperous than at any point in its history.
1499519467:
Lumber Consortium Camp

As of 4708 AR, the Lumber Consortium Camp in Andoran's Darkmoon Vale was the lumber cartel's main camp in the region. It specializes in harvesting copses of valuable duskwood.
1501056677:
Azad

The city of Azad is located on the northern shores of the Betul Sea in the Western Ghats region of Vudra. Despite being a city of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra, in terms of aesthetics, Azad is much closer in appearance to a Kelesh city as its architecture is dominated by beautiful domes and soaring minarets.
1515143497:
Mercenary League Headquarters

2 locations:
  • Passad's Mansion
  • Supplicant Properties
1518160677:
Oleg's Trading Post

Located about a dozen miles north of the Narlmarches on the southern edge of Brevoy, Oleg's Trading Post is an old border fort rebuilt to function as a trading post by Oleg Leveton and his wife Svetlana Leveton. The repurposed fort has seen mild financial success through hard work, visits from travelling merchants and a loan from Restov. However, the trading post is too remote and distant from any major waterways to reach its full potential—mainly relying on hunters, trappers, and a hermit named Bokken as their clientele.

Lately, the Stag Lord's bandits have taken notice of the trading post and regularly plunder its pitiful earnings. If not for his wife, the prideful owner would have already died fighting the bandits, but for her sake, he has requested reinforcements from Restov instead.
1525944977:
Hellirinn

The town of Hellirinn is located on the banks of the Rimeflow River a few miles downstream from Trollheim in the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is near a warm spring located in a cave that serves as an ancient Pharasmin shrine.
1526502567:
Wailing Cave

The Wailing Cave is a half-submerged cavern in the Pangalley Atoll which is avoided by the pirates of the isle, who claim the ghost of murdered Black-Eye Benbem haunts it.
1529314007:
Kho-Rarne Pass

The gap between the Barrier Wall mountains and the Brazen Peaks is known as the Kho-Rarne Pass. It links the northeastern Mwangi Expanse, near the Ruins of Kho, to Osirion, near to the Ruins of Rarne. The pass is wide and easily traveled, but due to Osirion's lack of interest in the Mwangi region, it is rarely used.
1546542037:
Heldren

The peasant village of Heldren is located in the Sophra Prefecture of southern Taldor, close to the Border Wood.
1559162477:
Castle Andachi

Castle Andachi is one of only two defensible positions on the Bleakwall that separates the Ustalavic county of Canterwall from the orc Hold of Belkzen. It is located west of Lake Lias in the southern foothills of the Tusk Mountains. The castle is presumably named after Count Andachi of Tamrivena who led a successful defense of Ustalav in the 41st century AR, before being betrayed by his head general, Kazavon. Castle Andachi is avoided by all, as it is known to be the lair of powerful creatures or haunts.
1570507387:
North Bridge

North Bridge, also known as Longriver Bridge, connects North Point and the neighbourhood of Northgate with mainland Varisia. North Bridge is one of the first sights that many people entering Korvosa see, as it connects Endrin Isle with the rest of Varisia and is one of the city's main entry points.
1574969847:
Willowshore

Willowshore is a logging village in the remote reaches of Shenmen, deep in the Specterwood forest.
1610278557:
Heavywater Perch

Heavywater Perch is a tiny site, little more than a single large rock near the southern end of Pangalley Atoll in the Shackles. The site's sole resident is a beautiful merfolk, Crimson Clepsana, with a tail of an enormous red snapper. She can usually be found sitting atop the rock when the Screaming Reaver is leaving or returning to port. The residents of Fort Benbem stay away from Heavywater Perch for fear of being enchanted by Clepsana's captivating song that lures many unwary sailors to their deaths. Captain Coal-Dark has informed her crew that anyone found staring at the sensuous merfolk while aboard her ship will receive a dozen lashes from her wicked cat-o'-nine-tails. As a precaution, she uses moist kelp to plug her crew's ears. Some of the crew wonder why Coal-Dark fails to simply take care of the seductress herself.
1610818247:
Claes

The Andoren fishing village of Claes is located on the Inner Sea coast, just over 50 miles south of the Andoshen River delta. A humble town, the inhabitants make their living from the ample harvest of the sea, supplementing it with some subsistence farming. When pirates or slavers attack, the people of Claes retreat to an ancient, underground Azlanti fortress known as the Claes Redoubt; the ruin is known to be quite deep.

General Reginald Cormoth, commander of Andoran's Eagle Knights, was born in Claes.
1625882917:
Temple of Gogunta

The Temple of Gogunta on Lady's Cape in southwestern Varisia, near the site of the Lady's Light, is a cavern considered to be a sacred place of the deity Gogunta by local boggards. As of 4712 AR, an informal village of boggards existed near the marshy pond about 200 yards east of the cavern. The cavern is one of several that connect to tunnels which run beneath the Lady's Lagoon.
1627195877:
Deadeye Lodge

2 locations:
  • Our Lady of Blessed Waters
  • Seerspring Garden
1629507397:
City of Bleeding Stones

The City of Bleeding Stones is the largest set of Ghol-Gan ruins on the island of Devil's Arches in the Shackles. It is nestled in a cove beside the island's vast, hilly rainforests. Many major expeditions have been sent to the City of Bleeding Stone, but each has ended in disaster, even in the expedition's group complete disappearance. The few survivors claim that some primordial evil plagues the ruins. The Pathfinder Society has expressed interest in exploring the city.
1630457437:
Consulate of the Platinum Band

2 locations:
  • Catwalk Corridors
  • Healing Raft
1633183127:
Stasis Fields

The Stasis Fields are a mysterious prison in the Bandu Hills. They consist of numerous underground caverns; in each cavern a solitary figure is held in stasis, floating in mid-air and turning slowly in time with the world's rotation.

Each prisoner wears turquoise scale armour and is armed with shining blades, giving rise to speculation that they are all prisoners of war from a long-forgotten army. Brave (or foolhardy) adventurers sometimes try to steal their equipment; no one knows what would happen if this caused a prisoner to awaken.

It was discovered in 4516 AR.
1636975207:
Genzei

Genzei, the City of Lanterns, is the capital of the Ushinawa Isles. It was established nearly two centuries ago, when samurai patriarch Tanagowu Useme conquered the islands.

The fiercely independent samurai and his rowdy followers fought the murderous kuru natives who populated the stony Ghol-Gani ruins that dotted the island. The bloody fighting lasted one month and, at the end, every kuru was put to the sword. The warriors and the Tian-Min sailors and servants who accompanied them established the new, limestone town of Genzei.

The settlers went to great efforts to erase any signs of the original Ghol-Gani builders, painting over the depictions of cannibalism and bloody alien rituals and removing the limestone carvings portraying their ravenous gods.
1641712797:
Sicnavier's Lair

Sicnavier's Lair is a dark chasm located in the Hungry Mountains in eastern Virlych, a grim mountain range in southern Ustalav.

A place haunted by the spirits of evil dragons, Sicnavier's Lair is always ruled by a sole umbral dragon who had succeeded the previous draconic master. The spoils of this rivalry is an enormous accumulation of wealth, lore, and slaves, more than enough to convince the successor to ignore ghostly whispers, even as the growing number of restless ghosts attempt to drive them insane.

Whichever dragon controls the lair names themselves 'Sicnavier', a title that has passed to no fewer than six dragons over the centuries. Attempts by outsiders to slay the dragon usually fail, but on those rare occasions when an attempt succeeds, a new, younger dragon takes on the mantle for him or herself.

The deepest pits of Sicnavier's Lair connects to the Darklands, although the dragon master and the creatures below have limited interaction with one another.

As of 4711 AR the ruler was Sicnavier VI, a very old umbral dragon who is said to have been driven to near madness from the tormenting whispers of its slain kin. Other inhabitants, such as Dark folk, nightwings, and tenebrous worms, also inhabit the lair and are most frequently slaves or pets of their draconic overlord.
1644281787:
Plain of Ten Thousand Swords

The Plain of Ten Thousand Swords in Numeria's Felldales is the haunted site of a bitter conflict from centuries past. The swords of thousands of Kellid warriors jut from the ground, each cursed with the bitter spirit of a warrior slain in the pointless war and bound to their weapon.
1646410227:
Laekastel

The Chelaxian city of Laekastel is located on northern shores of the Inner Sea, where the Brastle River flows into the Bay of Deng.
1651298687:
Golden Scarab

The Golden Scarab is a grand and magnificent building, residing in the shadows of the Dockside Giant in Katapesh. The walls of the Golden Scarab are made of alabaster and speckled with sparking seashells. Gold painted scarabs make their way across its surface, giving the building its name. The Scarab is perhaps the most famous gambling hall in the city, and its owner, Krebble-Jeggle adds new games to the hall at a breakneck pace. The selection of games includes roulette, cards, darts, dice games, and more exotic (and disreputable) games can be found upstairs in the Upper Hall.

The basement of the building is the home of the bloodiest games in all of Golarion: Mig-a-Mug-Tug, Hatch-a-Catch, Knivesies, and Killgull for example. Games involving undead are common as well; Zombie Melee, for example, is a "to the death" game where necromancers pit teams of zombies against each other.

Interestingly, there are no pit fights or duels to be found in the basement of the Golden Scarab. This is because the Pactmasters have granted the Guild of Gladiators sole rights to these sorts of sports.
1658514887:
Statue Street

Statue Street is a long stretch of road in the Absalom district of Westgate.

Appropriately named, the street contains dozens of scattered statues in various poses. These are the remnants of an Absalom tradition that has only recently been outlawed. In order to prevent prison overcrowding, nonviolent offenders were sometimes allowed to compete for a pardon in a race to outrun an enraged gorgon. The fortunate or skilful who evade the beast are granted a pardon for their crimes, while others now decorate the area with contorted expressions of shock, defiance, or terror.

Although ultimately considered unethical at best, there are many in the community who are upset that this tradition has been made illegal.
1658705517:
Castle Odranto

1 locations:
  • Ground of Lost Tears
1669307597:
Golden Cathedral

The Golden Cathedral is a former temple of Aroden located in the Liberty District of Andoran's capital city of Almas. Situated at the east end of the Field of Concord, this structure has been re-purposed as a library and repository of work dedicated to the concept of Common Rule.
1675068217:
Husanah

Husanah is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert, the barren region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Husanah is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean and is the southernmost city in Qadira. The city of Heger is the nearest large settlement and located north-west along the coastline.
1701915887:
Mount Mist

Mount Mist is the fifth-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 16,237 feet. It is located near the Five Kings Mountains village of Bowton, which also hosts base camps of climbers attempting to scale the mountain.
1722525617:
Severed Spiral

The Severed Spiral is a circle of 43 standing stones surrounding seven dolmens located deep within the Estrovian Forest of Mendev. The western half of the circle's stones are made of dark, rough-hewn basalt and other dark-colored stones, while the eastern stones are made of white, smooth dolomite.

The circle is the only permanent holy site of the Children of Balance, an organization of religious two-headed trolls led by Romrix who worship deities that most closely embody the duality and dichotomy of their lives—especially Pharasma, Nethys, and Gozreh.
1733060317:
Tents of Erukh

The Tents of Erukh is an encampment of impossibly beautiful people located within the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira. The camp is built around a massive tent owned by the exiled jaathoom wizard Erukh, for whom the camp is named. Erukh refuses to explain why he was banished from the Elemental Plane of Air to the Universe. He accepts no gifts from those looking to curry his favour and instead asks the supplicant to perform a task for him. The few who manage to achieve their assigned tasks are invited deeper into the Tents of Erukh, where supposedly even more wonders abound.
1741066207:
Corentyn

The Chelaxian city of Corentyn (pronounced KOHR-ehn-tin) is located on the far southwestern tip of the country, as the capital of the Archduchy of Longmarch. Founded as a military outpost, the Arch of Aroden forms the western border of the city, and a 50-foot-high limestone wall continues on around the northern and eastern landward sides. Also known as Port Indomitable and the City of Nine Forts, Corentyn has survived dozens of wars, and remains a major trade center and military outpost in the new, infernal Cheliax of House Thrune.
1748665567:
Longshadow

Longshadow is a large town in western Nirmathas dedicated primarily to processing the ore brought out of the nearby Hollow Hills and Mindspin Mountains and turning it into valuable commodities useful for Tamran or the Molthuni front.
1750458117:
Mount Kla

Mount Kla is the 12th-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 14,411 feet. It is located in the central parts of the range east of Emperor's Peak and west of the Verduran Forest and is visible from the sunlit East End of Highhelm's King's Crown layer.
1753247777:
Immaculate Repository

The Immaculate Repository is a highly visible temple of Abadar in the Dawn Gate district of Katapesh. It is packed with merchant stalls and tents, each of which hangs a decorative key in the front as homage to the Master of the First Vault. They also keep a tithe box, into which customers are expected to drop spare change following a transaction, and from which the clergy collects to fund the daily routines of the temple. Interestingly, none of the merchants take security precautions against theft from the tithe, as few would be foolish enough to rob a tithe box of Abadar in Katapesh, the city of merchants.
1755884947:
Bezin Hall

Bezin Hall once served as a school and orphanage dedicated to educating the community, but it was closed after its headmaster, Satriel Bezin, was executed following the discovery that they had abandoned their faith in the Midnight Lord.
1779788057:
Sunset Gate

Sunset Gate is one of the exits from Rego Cader, but since the district was abandoned long ago, this gate now leads to overgrown vegetation with a path that is not regularly used by anyone.
1783887817:
Valahuv

As of 4713 AR, Valahuv was a tiny town in Sarkoris that had resisted the invading demonic hordes of the Worldwound for over a century. Unfortunately, it drew the strength for its independence from its daemonic overlord Ommors, the Bringer of Twilight, who was worshiped as a goddess.
1810188527:
Zar Kragnaral

Zar Kragnaral is located in the Stormspear Mountains in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The legendary black basalt and blue ice fortress is ruled by the so-called Deathless Jarl.

Zar Kragnaral is an imposing structure nearly seven hundred feet in height. Its windows are made of hard ice and its towers are built of black rock. Delicate bridges of ice connect its black towers. The fortress looms atop a low plateau deep in the mountains. The view from its highest towers affords a panorama of the northern Thanelands and Hagreach.
1821736457:
Dome of the Savored Sting

2 locations:
  • Celwynvian Charge
  • Founder's Honor
1829794157:
House of the Beast

The House of the Beast is one of the most legendary temples dedicated to Rovagug in Katapesh. It is located on the barren slopes of the Pale Mountain in the Brazen Peaks. It is said that the temple had been built by a mysterious fire spirit who rose from the molten heart of the mountain.

As of 4709 AR it had become the lair of Ghartok, the Carrion King, warlord of the region's kholo tribes.
1837718577:
Marching Springs

The Marching Springs are a 30 square mile region of Nirmathas between the Fangwood and the Mindspin Mountains. Geysers erupt in a procession through the region.
1837887627:
Turtleback Ferry

1 locations:
  • Wreck of the Paradise
1842626657:
Ayajinbo

Ayajinbo is capital of the elven nation of Jinin in Tian Xia, and stewards of the Silver Tree, the Darklands entrance that elves used in millennia past to reach the continent. It is renowned for its gardens, well-crafted stone buildings, and extensive dawnsilver trade.
1850176017:
Viridian Forge

The Viridian Forge is a smithy made of basalt blocks that uses Netherworld fire to forge weapons. The blacksmith here is Ukorviak, a shadow giant and the only one among the Xoskerik tribe who knows the secrets of forging living steel. Weapon sales are usually reserved for agents of the Umbral Court, though Ukorviak sometimes decides to sell to foreigners, provided they pay the exorbitant prices he demands.

Kaer Maga

1859746867:
Seven's Sawmill

Seven's Sawmill is the oldest of the six major sawmills on Kyver's Islet in Magnimar and has never changed ownership. Production occurs here, but not in significant volumes, because it is used as the meeting place of the secret society called the Brothers of the Seven. Few know of the group or the meetings.
1864755467:
Court of Endless Revelry

The Court of Endless Revelry is the largest congregation of satyrs in the Palakar region, and is the closest thing to a satyr court.
1871171987:
Ihalar

Ihalar is an underground city located between the Obari Ocean and the Meraz Desert. It is the westernmost example of the Khattibi-style underground settlements. The residents emerge only to graze their herds and trade with passing merchants. The city boasts many underground gardens sustained by the city's light wells although the lowest levels of the city have quietly been abandoned.
1882045187:

Thronestep

Thronestep is a port city on the banks of Lake Encarthan, and the capital of the growing theocracy of Razmiran. A city of sharp contrasts, it is among the newest settlements in Avistan, having been built by the Living God's faithful less than 50 years ago.
1909748617:
Walcourt

Walcourt was a center of worship for followers of Founder Crucisal, but after Aroden's death the lodge was abandoned, and attempts to convert it into an orphanage have ended in accidents, leading it to be considered haunted.
1924099317:
Vugorak

Vugorak is a community of werecreatures hidden deep within the Hymbrian Forest on the Galtan bank of the East Sellen River. The community welcomes those who are werecreatures and gives them the opportunity to start a new life in the village.
1931091317:
Water Gardens of Xerbystes

The Water Gardens of Xerbystes were commissioned by Xerbystes I, the first Qadiran satrap of Osirion in the 16th century AR. Feeling that the delta of the River Sphinx was underutilized, the satrap ordered a complex of gardens and pools to be built on an island, and had it shored up against flooding. The new Keleshite elite frequented the garden, but it was avoided by the Osirians, who felt that the attempt to hold back the river was disrespectful to the water spirits. The river reclaimed the island in the great flood of 1535 AR, killing everyone who was there, and it has never been reoccupied. The island occasionally reemerges, leaving parts of the Gardens high and dry, but they are soon submerged in the Sphinx's floods again.
1933635507:
Elbistan

Elbistan is a small town in the western foothills of Taldor's Southern Range mountains.
1939702157:
Port Ice

Port Ice, on the southern bank of the Lake of Mists and Veils in northern Brevoy, is a refuge for ruffians and lake pirates. While the city was once the capital of the former nation of Issia, it has been part of greater Brevoy since 4499 AR, when Choral the Conqueror united the region with its southern neighbor, Rostland. Even when Choral made New Stetven capital of Brevoy, Port Ice remained the seat of power of the conniving pirate lords of House Surtova.

When Choral's descendants in House Rogarvia mysteriously disappeared to a man in 4699 AR, Noleski Surtova, then ruler of Port Ice, leapt at the opportunity and took control of the nation in the absence of its traditional sovereigns and has since moved his court to New Stetven to the south.

King Noleski's uncle, Domani Surtova, serves in the White Manor as Port Ice's current caretaker.

The Port is often overcome by ice and snow in the winter months and sits closed, only visited occasionally until the spring's thaw. The summer months are spent frantically stockpiling supplies for the next winter.
1944735787:
Five-Fire Pavilions

The Five-Fire Pavilions is a burnt-out structure with exposed blackened metal beams that can be considered Absalom's goblin embassy, ​​led by the explorer Skirma Toadlicker, who seeks to show that goblins are ready to join the "civilized" world.

Many goblins, hobgoblins, and others considered monsters have attended the pavilion's exhibitions, art displays, and fireworks, trading amongst themselves. However, not all cultural exchanges go smoothly, especially when a neighbor walks their dog, which some goblins interpret as a meat offering. There are also rivalries between tribes and differences between those who seek a more savage life, insulting hobgoblins who ride horses, and the more civilized, who call their less educated kin deformed. Even so, Skirma hopes that things will be better in the future and that this is a step forward.

Whale

The Whale is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. It stands 170 feet high. There are no known entrances into the piling.
1949808867:
Pleasure Salon of Calistria

Elves and aiuvarins are common among the temple prostitutes of the Pleasure Salon of Calistria in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom. Elves are otherwise relatively uncommon in Absalom, mostly consisting of adventurers or outcasts; more common are their half-elven descendants. Although Calistria's priests play a significant role in the operations of the temple, the Pleasure Salon is managed by the ranking sacred prostitute, Dyrianna of House Avenstar.
1970270947:
Cesca

Cesca is a settlement in eastern Ustalav, located in the county of Varno.
1981989857:
Market District

The Market District of Urgir, the de facto capital of the Hold of Belkzen, is located in the western side of the city's first tier just inside the western gate. It is the center of trade in Urgir and home to most of its non-orc residents, the majority of whom were invited by the late overlord Grask Uldeth's dromaar diplomats. Most of the market's permanent merchants were drawn out of desperation or because they traded in illegal goods, such as drugs, poisons, or slaves.
1984858297:
Zmeyka

Zmeyka is a town located in western Brevoy, southeast of the Golushkin Mountains. In 4717 AR, it became a flashpoint in the ongoing conflict between House Orlovsky and House Surtova over rulership of Brevoy.

2001771137:
Cold Marrow

The Cold Marrow is a haunted place in Darkmoon Wood that no living thing should approach. The temperature is lower in the area, and it's filled with dead trees that aren't empty of inhabitants. A feeling of being watched overwhelms those who venture inside. Monuments like white meheris forming staircases and symbols of extinct cultures could offer a revelation about the area, but no one is brave enough to take the risk.
2011751757:
Yavipho

The village of Yavipho is a tiny, predominantly gnome settlement in central Katapesh, very similar in nature to Tiven's Reed. It grew up around an oasis stopover for merchants and continues to serve as a place where one can sell almost anything to its curious inhabitants.

Constant harassment by slavers (especially from the kholo population) eventually forced the gnomes to migrate beneath the oasis, where homes and workshops are dug from the rock. Most dwellings have secret escape routes, and the settlement itself is carefully monitored by guards for fear of discovery from hostile outsiders. Few visitors are accepted into the community, and those select few are searched before allowed entry. Paranoia is the price of security.
2012393117:
Westpool

The small port towns of East Rikkan and Westpool, located north of Westcrown in Cheliax, are known collectively as the Rikkan Ports. These settlements house large numbers of merchants who trade goods up and down the River Adivian, and as of 4709 AR (prior to the abolition of slavery in Cheliax) also housed the numerous slaves required to operate the Stavian Arches and portage stations. Westpool is larger due to the presence of the Arch.

Since 4674 AR, Chelaxian forces frequently occupy the town looking for rebels and traitors. The current mayor of Westpool, Vismargrave of the Adivian Falling Tibalt Khrocini openly engages in politics and has gained much power and many enemies by controlling the transport route to Lake Sorrow and the capital Egorian.
2013476427:
Avrossi

Avrossi is a hamlet located 50 miles south of Mechitar, filled with estates granted to loyal servants of Gebbite leaders. From this unassuming place, Avrossi's mayor Furghol Rennold has been plotting against his own family for decades. Its population, consisting primarily of human wizards and clerics of Urgathoa as well as bodak, mohrg and ghoul wizards, employs mindless skeletons and zombie as field workers, and regularly receives human chattel from Corpseblight.
2019949917:
Ulm's Delve

Thap Samut

Thap Samut (pronounced: Tahp sahm-oot) is the northernmost city of the sorcerous nation of Tang Mai, located in the far north-east of the country. Thap Samut also serves as Tang Mai's largest port, where fortunes can be rapidly made and easily lost.
2028277937:
Moak Harbor

Moak Harbor was a port city on Shark Island in the Shackles that was home to more than 8,000 people at its peak more than 200 years ago, but today is only rotting ruins.

2084239417:
Bluestone

Bluestone is a hamlet on the border of Molthune and Nirmathas that frequently changes hands between the two nations. It was originally built around a circle of stones and a shrine to Gozreh. Its frequent changes in ownership has weakened the town's economy considerably, and the village no longer contains many elaborate structures outside the original Gozren shrine.
2105785807:
Forestheart

Forestheart is a powerful rift between Golarion and the First World. It is a dark glade in the ancient Grungir Forest surrounded by giant trees. In the center of the glade there is an otherworldly calm and clear pool. By observing the reflections in the water's surface a visitor could look at any place in the First World and, by leaping into the pool, he could be transported to the place the pool was reflecting. The journey is one-way and the visitor has to find another way back. A capricious and demanding nymph named Imavajana guards the pool and controls the destinations.
2107273757:
Gorum Pots

Gorum Pots are a site of abundant geothermal hot springs and mud pots located in the Numerian Plains region of central Numeria, near the source of the Dagger River. It is a traditional breeding ground for the dangerous insects known as zhen worms.
2108438017:
Azir

Azir, the capital of Rahadoum and the birthplace of the Laws of Mortality, is a bustling trade port whose appearance has been greatly modified recently.
2115901397:
Dead Slave Cove

Dead Slave Cove is a barren beach of Raptor Island, one of the westernmost islands of the Shackles, located near the mouth of the island's main river of Broken Hope. Sailors use the wild island to maroon troublesome enemies captured in battle as well as malcontents and traitors. Most castaways deposited on Dead Slave Cove are destined to spend the rest of their lives on Raptor Island. The rocky shores and the jagged reefs prevent ships from approaching Raptor Island, so a ship must anchor about a quarter-mile offshore and use a longboat to reach Dead Slave Cove.
2116232297:
Ghorvaul's Crossing

Ghorvaul's Crossing is a small pass through the Mindspin Mountains into Molthune and is guarded by the colossus Speakers of the Ehrotai.
2119198707:
Plains of Night

The Plains of Night is a ranch in the Atteran Ranches that is not run by the Atteran family or other regular ranchers. The ranch raises horses efficiently, and this ability has earned the respect of the locals, although there is suspicion surrounding the ranch workers, who are mostly foreigners.

It turns out that these suspicions are correct, as the owner, Etrixia Star-Touched, was a cleric of Desna, and her ranch serves as a base of operations for rebels. These rebels are called the Dreamtenders, who use rituals to transform the trauma of the Nidalese they help into dreamlike beings who seek revenge against the agents who caused their suffering.
2121868147:
Dauphenal Vineyard

The Dauphenal Vineyard has existed since Nidal was under Chelaxian occupation. The vineyard produces a highly sought-after wine called Dauphenal alvarino.
2127500987:
Grand Holt

The Grand Holt is a massive, multi-trunked fig tree located in the Green Ridge neighborhood of Absalom's Eastgate district. It is the oldest and largest tree on the Isle of Kortos, maybe even older than Absalom.

The tree is tended by a fanatical druidic sect known as the Circle of Stones, who also care for Iolanthe, the immortal dryad queen who lives within it. The Grand Holt has expanded greatly over the millennia of its life, and entire blocks of buildings have been demolished to facilitate its growth. Blights, such as those caused by the Welt and the Tyrant's Grasp, have not been able to deter the tree's growth, and in fact seem to cause it to grow stronger. The tree provides a refuge for nature-loving adventurers, who are hired by the Circle of Stones to further Iolanthe's cause within the city and beyond.
2144904107:
Katheer

Katheer, also known as "Glorious Golden Katheer", is the capital city of Qadira, and serves as the seat of power for the Satrap Xerbystes II.

The city possesses a canyon-like harbor and is the center of trade for goods to and from the distant Empire of Kelesh, as well as from throughout the Inner Sea. Each day, ships arrive from and depart for the major trading ports throughout the Inner Sea region, including Quantium, Absalom, and Katapesh. Katheer is also renowned as a center for learning, and contains dozens of academies of mathematics, philosophy, and healing.
2146042767:
Detmer

The main shipyard that powers the naval arm of Druma's mercantile empire, the city of Detmer is located on a rocky outcrop that is thrust out into the Gulf of Accord on Lake Encarthan. It is from here that the barges and caravels that cross the lake and ensure Druma's wealth are launched. The city is famous for its Sapphire Harbor which, thanks to Druma's incredible wealth, is a more literal description than most think. Very little trade (at least by Drumish standards) takes place in Detmer, as it is primarily focused on shipbuilding and maintenance. The Drumish Navy is headquartered here as well, providing security in the Gulf of Accord and wherever else Drumish merchant ships travel in the pursuit of trade.
